summary: Frontend-only Vajra prototype: exercised 8 requested flows on the deployed preview (mobile viewport 390x844, fresh browser context per session). Open-link/Pay-a-request, uncertain flow, activity deep-nav, wallet connect/disconnect, reduce-motion/transparency toggles, never-asks + how-passkey sheets, revoked filter, copy audit (no em-dash) and horizontal overflow check (all pages 390px) all PASS. Outage propagation is 3/4: home-outage-banner, pay-unavailable-card (no pay-button), and pay-banner-verified after OFF all pass, but activity-refresh-error is NOT rendered on /activity while outage is ON (root cause identified in activity.tsx). Create regression passes through step2, and step3 lock is correctly disabled with 'Set up Vajra Touch below…' reason on fresh contexts (no passkey). No React Native red screens or console errors observed (only the harmless useNativeDriver warning).

ui_bugs.issue: activity-refresh-error card does NOT appear on /activity when settings.outage is ON. The useEffect at lines 55-67 has an empty deps array ([]) and a stale closure over settings.outage; on first mount settings is still the default (outage=false) because VajraProvider hydrates async, so `if (settings.outage) setRefreshFailed(true)` inside the delayed callback reads the initial false. home-outage-banner and pay-unavailable-card work because those components read settings.outage in render, not in a one-shot effect. Reference: /app/frontend/app/activity.tsx:55-74

action_items: Fix activity.tsx so activity-refresh-error appears when outage is ON. Options: (a) depend on [hydrated, settings.outage] and re-run the check, or (b) read settings.outage via a ref, or (c) simply derive `const refreshFailed = !loading && settings.outage` (recommended — no closure/state coordination needed).

(Optional) Consider clearing refreshFailed automatically when settings.outage is toggled OFF while the user is on /activity — currently a hard refresh via pull-to-refresh is needed to clear it because the retry callback also depends on settings.outage.
critical_code_review_comments: activity.tsx useEffect: `[]` deps + delayed setState reading settings.outage is a stale-closure hazard even after the fix; prefer deriving refreshFailed from render state.
activity.tsx `refresh` callback (line 69) uses settings.outage from closure; this works today because it depends on [settings.outage], but the initial-mount effect above breaks the pattern.
seed_data_creation: None — all state is client-side (localStorage) and each browser context starts clean per product design.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Fresh mcp_browser_automation calls start with no passkey and no wallet. To test security-how-passkey-works / security-disable-passkey / security-disconnect-wallet you must first setup a passkey (goto /passkey-setup → click [data-testid=passkey-setup-button] → vajra-touch-sheet appears → [data-testid=vajra-touch-authenticate] → wait ≥3500ms for finishRegistration's 1100ms delay + ceremony's 1400+700ms to complete) and connect a wallet (security-connect-wallet → wallet-option-demo). Step1 continue is [data-testid=create-continue-terms] (NOT create-step1-continue); step2 review is [data-testid=create-review-request]; step3 lock is [data-testid=create-lock-authenticate]. never-asks sheet close testID is [data-testid=never-asks-close]. how-passkey close is [data-testid=how-passkey-close]. Uncertain flow's check-again card takes ~1.5s per press and stages resume for ~6-8s total, so use ≥25s wait for progress-final-seal after two clicks.
rca_of_issue: activity-refresh-error missing on outage: `useEffect(() => { delay(850).then(() => { if (settings.outage) setRefreshFailed(true); }); }, []);` at /app/frontend/app/activity.tsx:55-67 captures the initial `settings` object (outage=false from DEFAULT_SETTINGS in VajraProvider) because deps are `[]`. VajraProvider hydrates settings asynchronously (see /app/frontend/src/state/vajra.tsx:67-91), so by the time the 850ms delay resolves the state has been re-read, but the effect closure still holds the pre-hydration value. Home banner and Pay unavailable card do not have this issue because they subscribe to settings.outage during render. Repro: /security toggle-outage ON → /activity (wait 2s) → activity-refresh-error absent. Fix: derive `const refreshFailed = !loading && settings.outage` and drop the state + useEffect branch.
